title: ECG Quiz Question Bank — LITFL Sourced
description: Schema designed to capture ECG case data from LITFL's clinical cases database, including Top 150 ECG self-assessment cases and ECG Exigency clinical cases. Supports single-image interpretation, multi-part progressive Q&A, before/after ECG comparisons, and algorithm-based teaching cases.

total_cases.description: Number of top-level cases in this export.

total_questions.description: Sum of all questions across all cases.

target_audience.example: Final-year Physician Assistant students — PANCE/PANRE prep

cases.description: Each case represents one LITFL page. A case may contain one or more sub-cases (e.g., 'Metabolic Mayhem' has Case 1 and Case 2), and each sub-case may have multiple progressive questions.

case_id.description: Unique case identifier. Format: LITFL-ECG-XXXX for Top 150, LITFL-EX-XXXX for ECG Exigency, LITFL-CC-XXXX for other Clinical Cases.

source_url.description: Original LITFL page URL for this case.

source_type.description: Which LITFL collection this case comes from.

last_updated.description: Date the LITFL page was last updated.

keywords.description: Keywords from the LITFL clinical cases database searchable fields.

clinical_context.description: The overarching clinical vignette or narrative that opens the case. For Top 150 cases this is typically one line; for ECG Exigency cases this can be a multi-paragraph narrative.
clinical_context.example: Middle-aged patient presenting with chest pain and diaphoresis. BP dropped to 80/50 following sublingual nitrates.

sub_cases.description: Some LITFL pages contain multiple distinct ECG cases (e.g., 'Metabolic Mayhem' has Case 1: hypokalaemia and Case 2: hyperkalaemia). Single-case pages have one sub_case.

sub_case_label.description: Display label if multiple sub-cases exist.

sub_case_context.description: Clinical context specific to this sub-case (if different from the parent case context).
sub_case_context.example: A 30-year old female with a history of Conn syndrome secondary to bilateral adrenal adenomas presents with generalised weakness and muscle pains after a change in her medications.

media.description: All ECG images, rhythm strips, algorithm diagrams, and before/after comparisons for this sub-case.

media_id.description: Unique media identifier within this case.

type.description: Distinguishes ECG tracings from teaching diagrams and other media.

url.description: Direct URL to the image file on LITFL's CDN or local asset path.

alt_text.description: Accessible description of the image content.

caption.description: Caption or contextual note from the LITFL page, e.g. 'This patient also had STE in V4R, confirming the diagnosis of RV infarction' or 'Potassium now 5.5, T wave changes resolving'.

timing.description: When this ECG was taken relative to the clinical scenario. Supports before/after presentation.

questions.description: Progressive questions for this sub-case. LITFL typically uses 'Describe and interpret this ECG' as Q1, followed by diagnosis/management questions. Each question maps to an expandable 'Answer and Interpretation' section.

sequence.description: Order of this question within the sub-case (1-based). Questions are meant to be answered sequentially.

question_stem.description: The question as written on LITFL. May be open-ended ('Describe and interpret this ECG') or specific ('What is the diagnosis?').

question_format.description: Categorizes the question type based on LITFL's actual question patterns.

related_media_ids.description: Which media items from this sub-case are relevant to this question. References media_id values.

answer.description: The 'Answer and Interpretation' section from LITFL.

summary.example: Inferior STEMI with right ventricular infarction

ecg_findings.description: Structured list of ECG findings from the LITFL answer. Preserves the hierarchical structure (general findings, specific signs).

category.description: Grouping label from LITFL, e.g. 'General', 'Signs of inferior STEMI', 'Signs of associated right ventricular infarction'.

example: STE in inferior leads II, III, aVF
Reciprocal STD in lateral leads I, aVL, V6

interpretation_text.description: Full prose explanation from the LITFL answer, for cases where the answer is narrative rather than a findings list.

related_links.description: Links to LITFL reference pages embedded in the answer (e.g., links to 'inferior STEMI' or 'right ventricular infarction' library pages).

clinical_pearls.description: High-yield teaching points from the 'CLINICAL PEARLS' section. Not all cases have this.
example: RV infarction complicates 40% of inferior STEMIs
These patients are preload sensitive and may have an exaggerated hypotensive response to nitrates

references.description: Academic references cited on the LITFL page.

tags.description: Classification tags for quiz filtering and board-prep prioritization.
